subject: Geography
chapter: Solar Radiation, Heat Balance and Temperature

cases.passage: The city of Mumbai experiences an average temperature of 28°C throughout the year. During the summer months, the temperature rises to 38°C due to the high levels of solar radiation received from the sun. The heat balance in the city is maintained by the presence of the Arabian Sea, which helps to moderate the temperature. However, the city's heat island effect contributes to an increase in temperature. This effect is more pronounced in areas with high population density and lack of vegetation. The temperature in Mumbai is also affected by the presence of pollution, which traps heat and contributes to the urban heat island effect.

questions.q: Why does Mumbai experience a high temperature during the summer months?
opts: (A) Due to the presence of the Arabian Sea
(B) Due to the high levels of pollution
(C) Due to high levels of solar radiation received from the sun
(D) Due to the heat island effect
questions.explanation: Mumbai experiences a high temperature during the summer months due to the high levels of solar radiation received from the sun.

questions.q: How does the presence of the Arabian Sea affect the temperature in Mumbai?
opts: (C) It has no effect on the temperature
questions.explanation: The presence of the Arabian Sea helps to moderate the temperature in Mumbai.

questions.q: What contributes to the heat island effect in Mumbai?
opts: (B) High population density and lack of vegetation
(C) Low population density and lack of vegetation
questions.explanation: The heat island effect in Mumbai is contributed by high population density and lack of vegetation.

questions.q: How does pollution affect the temperature in Mumbai?
opts: (B) It has no effect on the temperature
(C) It traps heat and contributes to the urban heat island effect
questions.explanation: Pollution traps heat and contributes to the urban heat island effect, affecting the temperature in Mumbai.

questions.q: What is the average temperature in Mumbai throughout the year?
questions.explanation: The average temperature in Mumbai throughout the year is 28°C.

cases.passage: The desert region of Rajasthan experiences extreme temperature variations due to the heat balance and temperature conditions. During the day, the temperature can rise up to 45°C due to high levels of solar radiation. However, at night, the temperature can drop to 10°C due to the lack of vegetation and the presence of sand dunes. The heat balance in the region is affected by the presence of the Thar Desert, which has a high albedo value and helps to reflect solar radiation. This results in a decrease in the amount of heat received by the region.

questions.q: Why does the temperature in Rajasthan drop at night?
opts: (A) Due to the high levels of solar radiation
(B) Due to the presence of vegetation
(C) Due to the lack of vegetation and presence of sand dunes
(D) Due to the high albedo value of the Thar Desert
questions.explanation: The temperature in Rajasthan drops at night due to the lack of vegetation and presence of sand dunes.

questions.q: How does the albedo value of the Thar Desert affect the heat balance in Rajasthan?
opts: (A) It increases the heat received by the region
(B) It decreases the heat received by the region
(C) It has no effect on the heat balance
questions.explanation: The high albedo value of the Thar Desert helps to reflect solar radiation, resulting in a decrease in the amount of heat received by the region.

questions.q: What is the maximum temperature experienced in Rajasthan during the day?
questions.explanation: The maximum temperature experienced in Rajasthan during the day is 45°C.

questions.q: Why does the Thar Desert have a high albedo value?
opts: (A) Due to the presence of vegetation
(B) Due to the presence of sand dunes
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert has a high albedo value due to its desert nature.

questions.q: What is the minimum temperature experienced in Rajasthan at night?
questions.explanation: The minimum temperature experienced in Rajasthan at night is 10°C.
